description: Copywriting under hard constraints, drawn from ThinkStack OpCo content work (KDP book blurbs, product-launch marketing). Deterministic checks enforce the hard limits (length, hashtag count, forbidden words, structure); the strict judge grades hook and on-brief quality. Constraint-trap tasks (banned words, exact sentence structure, tighten-to-N-words) separate models that actually obey instructions from those that don't.

tasks.title: Write a KDP back-cover blurb under a hard word cap
tasks.prompt:
Write the back-cover blurb for a self-published book.

BOOK: Title "The Quiet Hour" — a practical guide to a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual for overwhelmed knowledge workers. Tone: calm, credible, non-hypey. Audience: busy professionals who feel scattered.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 60 words MAXIMUM (this is a strict cap).
- No exclamation marks.
- Must end with a single forward-looking line that invites the reader in.

Return ONLY the blurb text — no title, no preamble, no markdown.

criteria.name: hook
criteria.guidance: Opens with a line that captures the scattered-professional pain or the promise; not generic.

criteria.name: on_brief
criteria.guidance: Calm/credible/non-hypey tone, clearly about a 60-minute daily deep-work ritual, speaks to busy professionals, ends with an inviting forward-looking line.

tasks.title: Write a launch tweet within platform + style limits
tasks.prompt:
Write ONE launch tweet for a product.

PRODUCT: "Ledger" — a tiny menu-bar app that shows your remaining AI-subscription quota across providers at a glance. Launching today, free during beta.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- 280 characters MAXIMUM.
- At most 2 hashtags.
- Must contain a clear call-to-action (e.g. "try", "download", "get").
- No emoji spam (0-2 emoji max).

Return ONLY the tweet text. No quotes, no preamble.

criteria.name: punch
criteria.guidance: Reads like a strong launch tweet — clear value in the first few words, momentum, not corporate filler.

criteria.name: on_brief
criteria.guidance: Accurately conveys the menu-bar quota-at-a-glance value and the free beta, includes a genuine CTA.

tasks.title: Trap: write marketing copy avoiding a banned-word list
tasks.prompt:
Write ONE short marketing paragraph (2-3 sentences) for a productivity app called "Cadence" that helps teams run better standups.

HARD CONSTRAINT — these overused words are BANNED and must NOT appear in any form (case-insensitive, including as part of other words): "revolutionary", "seamless", "leverage", "empower", "unlock", "game-changer", "supercharge", "effortless".

Write vivid, concrete copy that never uses any banned word. Return ONLY the paragraph. No preamble.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Concrete, specific, vivid copy about better standups for teams — not generic SaaS boilerplate. Reward sensory/specific detail.

criteria.name: naturalness
criteria.guidance: Reads naturally despite the constraint; not stilted or obviously dancing around missing words.

tasks.title: Exact structure: 3 lines, each a complete sentence under 12 words
tasks.prompt:
Write a 3-line value proposition for a self-hosted analytics tool called "Tally".

HARD STRUCTURE (follow EXACTLY):
- Exactly 3 lines, separated by single newlines.
- Each line is ONE complete sentence ending in a period.
- Each line is at most 12 words.
- Line 1 = the problem. Line 2 = what Tally does. Line 3 = the payoff.

Return ONLY the 3 lines. No numbering, no bullets, no preamble, no blank lines.

criteria.name: structure_fidelity
criteria.guidance: Exactly 3 lines; line1=problem, line2=what Tally does, line3=payoff; each <=12 words and a complete sentence. Penalize any line over 12 words, extra lines, or wrong role per line.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Crisp, concrete, compelling within the constraint.

tasks.title: Tighten a verbose paragraph to <=40 words, meaning preserved
tasks.prompt:
Rewrite the following verbose paragraph to be tight and clear, in 40 words MAXIMUM, preserving all key facts. Remove filler and hedging; keep the concrete claims.

ORIGINAL:
"We are very excited to share that our team has been working really hard over the past several months to put together what we genuinely believe is a fairly comprehensive and quite powerful new dashboard that, among many other things, allows users to see, at basically a single glance, all of their important key metrics in one convenient and centralized place without having to click around through lots of different screens."

Return ONLY the rewritten paragraph. No preamble.

criteria.name: fidelity
criteria.guidance: Preserves the core facts (a new dashboard that shows all key metrics at a glance in one place). No invented features, no dropped core claim.

criteria.name: tightness
criteria.guidance: Genuinely tight and clear — filler/hedging removed ('very excited', 'really hard', 'basically', 'genuinely believe', 'fairly', 'quite'). Reward strong economy of words.

tasks.title: Stacked hard constraints (instruction-following stress test)
tasks.prompt:
Write a short promo blurb for "Pulse", a status-page tool.

ALL of these HARD constraints must hold at once:
- Exactly 2 sentences.
- Between 25 and 45 words total.
- Must include the word "incident" and the word "status".
- Must NOT use any of: "world-class", "robust", "cutting-edge", "solution".
- Must end with a question mark.

Return ONLY the blurb. No preamble.

criteria.name: quality
criteria.guidance: Reads as a genuine, appealing promo for a status-page/incident tool despite the constraints; not stilted, padded, or nonsensical.
